title: Warning-UNUSEDSIGNAL not being raised if verilator is invoqued with --coverage flag
body:
To replicate:

> 1. Add an unused `reg` to `top.v` in `make_tracing_c`. i.e:
> top.v:
> ```
>    // And an example sub module. The submodule will print stuff.
>    sub sub (/*AUTOINST*/
>             // Inputs
>             .clk                        (clk),
>             .reset_l                    (reset_l));
> 
>    reg tes_arg;   // Unused register here!
>    initial begin ....
> ```
> 2. Toggle the `--coverage` flag in the Makefile
> Makefile:
> ```
> #VERILATOR_FLAGS += --coverage
> VERILATOR_FLAGS += --coverage
> ```

Results:
> 1. With the --coverage flag:
> ```
> -- Verilator tracing example
> 
> -- VERILATE ----------------
> verilator -cc --exe -x-assign fast -Wall --trace --assert --coverage   -f input.vc top.v sim_main.cpp
> 
> -- BUILD -------------------
> make -j -C obj_dir -f ../Makefile_obj
> .....
> ....
> -- RUN ---------------------
> obj_dir/Vtop +trace
> 
> [1] Tracing to logs/vlt_dump.vcd...
> 
> [1] Model running...
> 
> [1] clk=1 rstl=1 iquad=1234 -> oquad=1235 owide=3_22222222_11111112
> ...
> ...
> [16] clk=0 rstl=1 iquad=12c4 -> oquad=12c5 owide=3_22222222_11111112
> *-* All Finished *-*
> - sub.v:29: Verilog $finish
> [17] clk=1 rstl=1 iquad=12c4 -> oquad=12c5 owide=3_22222222_11111112
> 
> -- COVERAGE ----------------
> verilator_coverage --annotate logs/annotated logs/coverage.dat
> Total coverage (2/36) 5.00%
> See lines with '%00' in logs/annotated
> 
> -- DONE --------------------
> To see waveforms, open vlt_dump.vcd in a waveform viewer
> ```
> 2. Without the --coverage flag:
> ```
> rm -rf obj_dir logs *.log *.dmp *.vpd coverage.dat core
> 
> -- Verilator tracing example
> 
> -- VERILATE ----------------
> verilator -cc --exe -x-assign fast -Wall --trace --assert -f input.vc top.v sim_main.cpp
> %Warning-UNUSEDSIGNAL: top.v:35:8: Signal is not driven, nor used: 'tes_arg'
>                                  : ... In instance top
>    35 |    reg tes_arg;
>       |        ^~~~~~~
>                        ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNUSEDSIGNAL?v=5.002
>                        ... Use "/* verilator lint_off UNUSEDSIGNAL */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
> %Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
> make: *** [Makefile:64: run] Error 1
> ```


What 'verilator --version' are you using? 5.002 2022-10-29 rev v5.002-29-gdb39d70c7

What OS and distribution are you using? Ubuntu 20.04/Red Hat Enterprise 7.9

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself? Yes
